item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simply not very interesting in my opinion. The image depicted is very reminiscent of generic aliens so no real wow factor there. It supposedly holds unknown knowledge of the past but the only things that it has divulged are 2 meaningless languages that aren't really talked about much at all. It's personality is of a posh English person who's smart which is a huge cliche. The whole interaction with light just seems tacked on. I'm sorry but this scip is just to devoid of personality for me to even give it a neutral vote. -1

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This image is non-compliant because Victoria and Alberts Museum, from which this image originates from, only permits limited non-commercial usage of their image. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I imagine is a lawn gnome.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: As the author of this article, I approve the proposed crosslinks.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show Proposal
- Hide

To link to articles involving artificial beings and English anomalous activity, I propose the following edit.

Terribly sorry." SCP-030 delivers this response in the same tone and cadence each time any question regarding its origins or creator are presented.

to

Terribly sorry." SCP-030 delivers this response in the same tone and cadence each time any question regarding its origins or creator are presented. Given its composition and location of origin, a link to the Alchemists of Alagadda is suspected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed the read but am left a bit baffled. They seemingly found the minimum amount of light for this SCP to remain active but what is with the 2000 lumen upper limit? I can understand taking away any light making tools it makes for itself but what happens if more light is allowed? Duplication? Rampancy? Evolution?

If the thing acts the same way for 10 minutes of darkness vs 10 days or more, has it noticed a change in speech or clothing in the people it meets? We certainly don't dress or talk the same way we used to. It is possible we're sending in our best scientists who also LARP for the time period but I see no mention of it in the containment requirements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite part of all about this is the last paragraph describing the initial discovery of the skip. That's what earned my upvote. Tiny coffin opens up and a little artificial man sits up and simply says "good afternoon" to everybody shitting their pants around it. It's so adorably polite!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. Didn't see it before the rewrite, but it's good now. I agree the SCP is pretty loose for it, but I feel the reasons for this are duly explained, and that the researchers are appropriately paranoid about it.
The one thing that does bug me, though… just by virtue of being in a containment site, isn't it surrounded by things like incandescent light bulbs, fluorescent light tubes, possibly LEDs on things like cameras or locks, stainless steel, plastic, and other things that it doesn't know about and might supernaturally reverse-engineer? It seems to me that exposing it to any modern tech is a problem (built its doo-dad by hearing mention of new tech), and there's nothing in the containment procedures mentioning this. I'm okay with it being around these because it's too late… it was exposed before its abilities were cataloged, but I feel that if that's the case, it should be documented.
Still, though, I upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A humanoid SCP that is not complicated, but still charming. Really hard to pull off. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm liking this one. I'm still pretty new, so I don't know if it's been referenced or "used" elsewhere on the site or with other SCPs, but this one doesn't seem to be as "spread around" as general knowledge, if you will, like ones such as 682, 914, 500, 076, and some others.

I can see where you wouldn't want 030 to just start solving scientific mysteries all over, but I like the idea of it being utilized more since it appears to be very cooperative with the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the changes. They've addressed all of my concerns. +1 earned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never read this before so I don't know the changes made but it seems to read well.

My only thought is that the "Addendum 1: Hide Document 030-C: Security Logs for SCP-030" at the bottom about it not getting requested items anymore goes against what was said in the procedures and it should probably be noted in the "Special Containment Procedures:" that that has changed.

Otherwise you read it and it says you can give it whatever it requests and then you read the full thing and too late you already gave it stuff!

/nitpicking

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Modernized the fossil a bit. I feel he's lost some charm, but hopefully the Foundation is better represented to current standards as requested.

I imagined I'd still get the question "Why does it get to make requests for research things?! It is a thing that doesn't get to make requests! Grr!", so I attempted to address that. It is permitted to do (strictly monitored) research because occasionally, it researches using methodologies that are either completely unknown or extensions of theories that were supposedly abandoned.

It may also be (again, under very strict circumstances) "checked out" like a tiny, outdated, and man-shaped encyclopedia to look at things from its unique perspective and offer insight. This is the accommodation made to eliminate its much-maligned formerly free-roaming nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be looking into changes with some of the suggestions presented in mind. Not real fond of the idea that everything needs to be "fixed" in one specific way, but the tone and the times have changed and this certainly could use an update.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think azzleflux's line by line above is just about everything that should be changed; maybe fix them, but other than that… I don't think there should be a couple hundred scips free-roaming, but if there was one or two that everybody knew, it could join the ranks of 914, 682, 231, and 076.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: That'd be excellent.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Eugh. Painful, that. :)

If we're initiating yet another cleanup, I'm happy to edit my old fossils to the current standards.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: The Containment Procedures definitely need to be rewritten. Eugh.
This one needs a lot of work to bring it up to date I think, but I like it well enough. Congrats.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

SCP-030 is confined to Site 62 with a special security grade (Class F) allowing it access to all unsecured research labs, records facilities and communal social areas (mess halls, dormitories, gymnasiums) when accompanied by at least one (1) staff member Class 1 or higher.

So, it's a roamer.

With the written consent of Level 4 personnel, SCP-030 may be granted temporary access to secured research labs or Euclid-class SCP containment facilities to act as a research assistant under the direct supervision of assigned laboratory staff.

…And also participates in the laboratory. Even though it's an anomalous entity. Hm.

SCP-030 was discovered 6/12/████ on the grounds of the cemetery of ██ ████ █████████'s Church in London's Mortlake District. It was buried in an unmarked plot approximately 2.7 meters (9 feet) in depth, contained in a small stone sarcophagus.

…How? Who the hell was just digging up a cemetery and happened to find this?

The sarcophagus lid was shattered by a careless local historian during the excavation, exposing SCP-030 to daylight.

I didn't know that historians were also graverobbers.

Its solid blue eyes lack discernible irises or pupils, rather resembling two small cut sapphires.

Tone, I think.

To date, SCP-030 has been responsible for no less than 6000 hours of research assistance to Site 62 staff across multiple departments and command levels. Despite the opportunity, SCP-030 has shown no desire to escape the confines of Site 62, regularly reporting that it "has plenty to help with around here, thank you."

I really, really don't like the idea of this.

6/16/████: SCP-030 has requested materials and lab space for independent research. Approval pending.

Why has it been put in such a position that it is even possible for it to request this?


I personally dislike this article, due to the fact that I feel the procedures described are uncharacteristic of the Foundation. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Artificial butler: just add sunlight!

I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you want to make it seem more official, and like an impersonal research document, I advise re-writing the required amount of Light to Lumins rather than the slightly obscure 'small candle' sort. :D

item.title: Re: Seriouse offer
item.content:encoded:
I know they left I was just voicing what I wanted to say on that convorsation

And I do not want to edit this page I want to make a foundation tale about it.

Thnx for the advice though :)

item.title: Re: Seriouse offer
item.content:encoded:
I will take that challenge if you want to read what I've got and help out

Dude did you see the dates on the posts you're replying to :P I suspect those two gentlemen and/or ladies are long gone by now.

You can't add anything to this article without author permission or mod/admin permission (the latter of which is unlikely to be granted considering the article is doing fine as it is, and that sounds like more than just a minor edit for cleanup).

But if you want to do a story, no one's stopping you. You can get feedback from chat or the forums, and either way, once you post it, it will get feedback alright, good or bad.

item.title: Seriouse offer
item.content:encoded: I will take that challenge if you want to read what I've got and help out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one's good. Few SCPs can be called 'likeable' and still work, but this one does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's why he's called "Ariel", obviously. Look up "The Tempest".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He reminds me of a golem, really, only British in origin rather than Jewish. Like Dexanote said, he's more of a sentient tool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If he controlled flame and could win at everything forever, yeah, Mary Sue. As it is, he's more of a sapient tool than anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I'd say that this one, while certainly likeable, is not a mary sue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems more than just a little Mary Sue-ish. Am I missing something?

item.title: Origins
item.content:encoded:
It'd be interesting to see what this one knows about Shakespeare.

It's also quite curious that it has such advanced knowledge of chemistry and physics, since so much of what we know in those fields is barely 150 years old. That it can learn extremely rapidly and quickly obtain such knowledge, okay. But that it already had them?

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: I agree with WillDrell, especially in the form of a story..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like to see more done with this one, that lab should be approved.
